note: LATEST BLOCKS profile column now distinguishes the three mechanisms that can ease a profile post-fork. Until v41 a label like 'H9 -> E1' was misleading because it implied the staged-relief cascade alone explained the gap; in fact for block 6565 staged relief alone would have produced B0 and the extra notch came from lag-clamp / anti-stall. The new display renders three values when the actual profile is below the staged-only floor: 'H9 -> B0 -> E1' (raw cASERT base, staged-relief-only floor, actually committed). When staged alone explains the gap, two values are shown. When all three coincide (pre-fork or fully aligned), a single value. Constants in the JS mirror include/sost/params.h CASERT_STAGED_* and must be kept in sync if the chain ones change. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
